The Syrians and Assyrians first ravaged the countries mentioned here, and that region was initially favored by the preaching of Christ. Those who desire the gospel walk in darkness and are in great danger. But when the gospel comes to a place or a soul, light comes with it. Let us earnestly pray that it may shine into our hearts and make us wise for salvation. The gospel brings joy. Those who want joy must expect to go through hard work, like a farmer before he has the joy of harvest; and hard conflict, like a soldier before he divides the spoils. The Jews were delivered from the yoke of many oppressors; this was a shadow of the believer's deliverance from the yoke of Satan. The cleansing of believers’ souls from the power and pollution of sin will be by the influence of the Holy Spirit, as purifying fire. These great things for the church shall be done by the Messiah, Emmanuel. The Child is born; it was certain; and the church benefitted by his undertaking before Christ came in the flesh. It is a prophecy of him and of his kingdom, which those who waited for the Consolation of Israel read with pleasure. This Child was born for the benefit of us men, of us sinners, of all believers from the beginning to the end of the world. He is justly called Wonderful, because he is both God and man. His love is a wonder to angels and glorified saints. He is the Counsellor, because he knew the counsels of God from eternity; and he gives counsel to men, in which he considers our welfare. He is the Wonderful Counsellor; no one teaches like him. He is God, the mighty One. Such is the work of the Mediator that no less power than that of the mighty God could bring it to pass. He is God, one with the Father. As the Prince of Peace, he reconciles us to God; he is the Giver of peace in the heart and conscience; and when his kingdom is fully established, men shall learn war no more. The government will be upon him; he will bear the burden of it. Glorious things are spoken of Christ’s government. There is no end to its increase, because the happiness of its subjects will last forever. The exact agreement of this prophecy with the doctrine of the New Testament shows that Jewish prophets and Christian teachers had the same view of the person and salvation of the Messiah. To what earthly king or kingdom can these words apply? Give then, O Lord, to your people knowledge of you by every endearing name, and in every glorious character. Give increase of grace in every heart of your redeemed upon earth.

Those are ripening quickly for ruin whose hearts are not humbled under humbling providences. For that which God designs when he smites us is to turn us to himself; and if this point is not gained by lesser judgments, greater may be expected. The leaders of the people misled them. We have reason to be afraid of those who speak well of us when we do wrong. Wickedness was universal; all were infected with it. They will be in trouble and see no way out; and when men’s ways displease the Lord, he makes even their friends to be at war with them. God would take away those they thought had help from him. Their rulers were the head. Their false prophets were the tail and the rush, the most despicable. In these civil contests, men preyed on near relatives who were as their own flesh. The people do not turn to Him who smites them, therefore he continues to smite; for when God judges, he will overcome; and the proudest, stoutest sinner shall either bend or break.
